originalTitle: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
title: Homes and Promises: A Story from Nigeria!
content:
Hi everyone! It's your friendly reporter, coming to you from sunny Africa! Today, we have a very important story from a big country called Nigeria. Nigeria is like a giant in West Africa! Our story is about homes, big cities, and promises.

Meet Nigeria!

Nigeria is a very, very big country. It's in West Africa, right next to the sea. Lots and lots of people live there. In fact, Nigeria has more people than any other country in all of Africa! Imagine so many children, mums, and dads all living in one country. Did you know? Over 200 million people call Nigeria home! That's a super big number! Nigeria is known for its amazing music that makes you want to dance. They also have super tasty food and people who love to laugh and play.

What is a Capital City?

Every country has a special city called its capital. This is a very important city. It's like the main office for the whole country. In the capital city, important people work. This includes the country's leader, called the president, and other people who help run the country. They make big plans for everyone. Did you know? Before the current capital, another busy city called Lagos was Nigeria's capital. Lagos is still a very, very busy place today!

A New Home for Nigeria

Many years ago, the leaders of Nigeria decided something big. They wanted to build a brand new capital city. They wanted this city to be special and right in the middle of Nigeria. This new, important city is called Abuja. It is a beautiful city now, with wide roads and tall, shiny buildings. Building a big city like Abuja takes a lot of land and space.

Where Did the City Grow?

Before Abuja was built, many families already lived on that land. They had their homes, their small villages, and their farms there. They grew food like yams, maize, and beans. They lived peaceful lives, playing with their friends and working on their farms every day. Their families had lived on that land for many, many years, even their grandmas and grandpas before them.

What Does 'Evicted' Mean?

Imagine you are playing in your house, and someone comes and tells you, "You need to leave your house! We need this land for something else." That's a bit like what happened to these families. The government needed the land to build the new city, Abuja. So, they asked the people living there to leave their homes and move somewhere else. This is called being 'evicted'. It can be very sad and scary to leave your home. You leave your friends, your school, and the land where you played and grew up.

Promises, Promises!

When the government asked these families to move, they made some very important promises. A promise is like saying, "I will definitely do this for you!" It's a very serious thing to say. The government promised to give these people new homes. They also promised new land for farming, so the families could still grow their food. They said they would help them start new lives. They even promised new schools for the children to learn in. And new clinics, which are like small hospitals, for when people get sick. These were big and important promises. Did you know? Keeping promises is super important! When you keep your promises, people trust you. When you don't, people can get very sad and lose trust.

What Happened Next?

The families believed the government. They packed their few things and moved away from their homes and farms. They trusted that the promises would come true. Abuja slowly grew into a big, shiny city. Many, many people came to live and work in this new capital. It looked very modern and busy. But for many of the families who moved, things did not go as planned. They say that a lot of the promises were not kept. It was very disappointing.

Life Was Hard

Imagine moving to a new place and not having a proper home. Or not having enough land to grow food to feed your family. That's what happened to many of these people. Many families became very poor. They found it hard to get food and good water. Their children could not always go to the new schools that were promised. They felt very sad and forgotten. Their old homes were gone forever, and the new life they were promised did not happen. It was a very tough time for them.

We Want Our Voice!

Now, after many years, these people are speaking up. They are saying, "We want our voice to be heard!" Having a voice means you can tell people what you think and how you feel. It means you want to be listened to, especially by your leaders. They want the government to remember them. They want the government to keep those old promises. They want to be part of the good things happening in Nigeria too. Did you know? Everyone deserves a voice! It's important for everyone to be able to share their thoughts and feelings, especially about their homes and families.

Why Is This Important?

This story reminds us that every person's home and family are very, very important. It teaches us that promises should always be kept, especially by big leaders who run the country. When people work together and listen to each other, things can become much better for everyone. We hope the leaders of Nigeria will listen to these families and help them now. Every child and every family needs a safe and happy home. And every promise made should be a promise kept. Let's hope for a happy ending to this story for these Nigerian families!
summary: Many years ago, people in Nigeria had to leave their homes to build a new big city, and now they say the government did not keep its promises to help them.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone needs a home, and leaders should always keep their promises to their people to build trust and fairness.

originalTitle: Burkina Faso must 'forget' about democracy, military leader says
title: A Special Way to Lead in Burkina Faso!
content:
Hello, My Little Friends from Africa!

Jambo, jambo! It's your friendly news reporter, here to share some exciting (and important!) news from our amazing continent, Africa. Today, we're going on a little trip to a country called Burkina Faso. Can you say that with me? Bur-ki-na Fa-so! It's a beautiful place with lots of friendly people, just like where you live.

What is Burkina Faso?

Burkina Faso is a country in West Africa. It's like a big home for many, many families. The name "Burkina Faso" means "Land of Upright People." Isn't that a lovely name? It means people who are honest and strong. The capital city of Burkina Faso is Ouagadougou. That's a fun one to say! It sounds like "Waga-doo-goo." * **Did you know?** Burkina Faso is famous for its delicious mangoes and for its special fabrics with bright colors!

Who is in Charge There Now?

In Burkina Faso, there is a leader named Captain Ibrahim Traoré. He used to be a soldier, which means he worked to keep people safe. He wears a uniform, just like the brave police officers or security guards you might see. Last year, in 2023, Captain Traoré became the leader of the country.

What Did Captain Traoré Say?

Captain Traoré said something very interesting. He said that right now, for Burkina Faso, the way of "democracy" might not be the best way to lead the country. He thinks his way might work better for them right now. This is a big thing to say, so let's try to understand what he means.

What is Democracy? (And Why Do People Like It?)

Now, what is "democracy"? It's a big word, but it's a simple idea! Democracy is when everyone in a country gets to choose their leaders. It's like when you and your friends vote for which game to play, or who gets to be the line leader at school. Everyone gets a say! When people vote, they choose someone they trust to make good rules for their country. The leaders listen to the people. This way, the country is led by the people, for the people. Many countries around the world use democracy because it gives everyone a voice. It helps people feel important and heard. * **Did you know?** The word "democracy" comes from very old words that mean "people power"!

What is a Military Leader?

Captain Traoré is a military leader. This means he comes from the army, which is a group of people who are trained to protect the country. Soldiers learn to be strong, disciplined, and quick to act. Sometimes, in some countries, a soldier becomes the leader. When a military leader is in charge, they often make decisions very quickly. They might feel that making fast decisions is important, especially if the country has big problems that need to be fixed right away. They want to make sure everyone is safe and secure. * **Did you know?** Soldiers wear special uniforms to show they are part of the army! These uniforms help them work together.

Why Would He Say No to Democracy?

So, why did Captain Traoré say that democracy might not work right now for Burkina Faso? Well, sometimes, countries face big challenges. Maybe there are problems with people not feeling safe, or not having enough food, or needing better schools. Captain Traoré might believe that with his way of leading, he can fix these problems faster. He might think that if everyone waits to vote, it will take too much time. He wants to make changes quickly to help his people be safe and happy. He feels that a strong leader, like himself, can make the country strong very fast.

What Does Burkina Faso Really Need?

No matter who is leading, every country needs some important things to make its people happy and healthy. Burkina Faso needs peace, so people can live without fear. It needs safety, so children can go to school and play. It needs good food, clean water, and doctors to help sick people. It also needs good schools, so all the children can learn and grow up to be smart, kind, and helpful citizens. Everyone wants to live in a place where they feel safe, loved, and have opportunities to learn and play. * **Did you know?** Many countries in Africa are working hard every day to build more schools and hospitals for their people!

What Happens Next?

People all over the world, including your friends and family in Africa, are watching Burkina Faso. They hope that Captain Traoré and his team will work hard to bring peace, safety, and happiness to all the people there. They want to see children smiling, learning, and playing freely. It's important for leaders to listen to their people and help them build a wonderful future.

Our Hopes for Burkina Faso!

We wish all the people of Burkina Faso lots of peace and success. We hope they find the best way forward to make their country a place where everyone can thrive. Let's send them our good wishes!
summary: Captain Ibrahim Traoré, who is the leader of Burkina Faso, said that his country should not use democracy right now, and he believes his way of leading will make people safe and happy faster.
whyMatters: This matters because it means the people of Burkina Faso might not get to choose their leaders by voting, and it's important for everyone to have a say in their country's future and to feel safe and happy.

originalTitle: US deports eight people 'of African origin' to Uganda
title: People Travel Home: A Story About Rules and Kindness
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hello, my super friends! It's your news buddy here, ready to share a story from our big, beautiful continent of Africa and beyond! We live in Africa, a land full of sunshine, amazing animals, and wonderful people, right? Did you know that there are many, many countries all over the world? Like Uganda, which is a lovely country right here in Africa! And then there's a big country far away called the United States of America, or USA for short. Sometimes, people travel from one country to another. Maybe to visit family, or to learn new things, or to find a new place to live. It's like when you visit your auntie's house, but much, much further away! When people travel to another country, there are always special rules. These rules are very important, like the rules for playing a game fairly. Rules help everyone stay safe and happy.

What is 'Deportation'?

Now, let's learn a new big word: 'Deportation'. Don't worry, it's just a fancy way of saying something simple. Imagine you are visiting a friend's house. Your friend's parents have rules, right? Maybe 'no running inside' or 'clean up your toys'. If someone goes to live in another country, like the USA, they need special papers and permissions. It's like needing a special ticket to get into a big amusement park. These papers show that they are allowed to be there. Sometimes, if someone doesn't have the right papers, or if they stay for too long, the country might ask them to go back to their home country. This is what 'deportation' means – sending someone back to their own country. In our story, eight people who came from countries in Africa were in the USA. And the USA government decided to send them back to Uganda. Did you know? People travel for many reasons! Some travel to study, some to work, and some to find a safe place if their home is not safe anymore.

People Going Back to Uganda

So, eight people who are 'of African origin' were sent back to Uganda. 'Of African origin' just means their families came from Africa, maybe a long, long time ago, or they were born there. Uganda is a beautiful country in East Africa, known for its friendly people and amazing wildlife. It has big lakes and green forests. Many people visit Uganda to see special animals like gorillas and chimpanzees! The eight people were flown on a plane all the way from the USA back to Uganda. It's a very long flight, almost a whole day! Imagine sitting on a plane for so long! Did you know? Uganda is sometimes called the 'Pearl of Africa' because it's so beautiful and precious!

Why Some People Are Not Happy

Now, this is where our story gets a little bit serious. A very important group of people in Uganda called the 'Uganda Law Society' heard about this. They are like super smart detectives for rules! The 'Uganda Law Society' is like a big team of smart people who know all about laws and rules. They make sure everyone is treated fairly and that rules are followed by everyone, even governments. They said that sending these eight people back to Uganda might not have been fair. They used a big word: 'criticises'. This means they were not happy about it and they spoke out about it very loudly. They said it was 'illegal'. This means they thought it didn't follow the right rules or laws that countries should follow when sending people home. Like breaking a rule in a game. They also said it was 'dehumanising'. This is a very important word. It means treating people in a way that makes them feel like they are not important or not real people. It's like making someone feel very, very sad and without hope. It's not a kind way to treat anyone. Did you know? Everyone, everywhere, deserves to be treated with kindness and respect, no matter where they come from, or what country they are in!

Why Rules and Kindness Matter

The Uganda Law Society wants to make sure that even when countries send people back home, they do it in a kind and fair way. They want everyone to follow the rules of treating people well. They believe that everyone should be treated with dignity. 'Dignity' means treating someone in a way that shows they are valuable and important. It means remembering that they are people with feelings. Imagine if someone was sent away from their home without their toys or without saying goodbye to their family. That would be very sad, right? The Law Society wants to prevent people from feeling like that. Laws and rules are there to protect all of us. They help us to live together peacefully and to make sure everyone is safe and happy. Rules are for fairness and safety. So, when the Uganda Law Society says something is 'illegal' or 'dehumanising', it means they think someone might not have followed the rules of kindness and fairness. They are standing up for what they believe is right. It's a reminder for all of us, no matter where we live, to always be kind to others. And to remember that people who travel far away might need extra help and understanding because they are in a new place. Did you know? There are special rules called 'human rights' that say everyone has the right to be treated well and fairly, no matter who they are or where they live in the world!
summary: Eight people from Africa were sent back to Uganda from the USA, and a group of lawyers in Uganda said it was not fair or kind because rules were not followed and people were not treated well.
whyMatters: This is important because everyone, everywhere, deserves to be treated with kindness and fairness, even when rules about travel are being followed, and special groups like the Uganda Law Society help to make sure this happens.

originalTitle: Iran executes two convicted members of banned opposition group
title: Sad News from a Faraway Land: Learning About Rules and Countries
content:
Hello, my lovely friends across Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here with an important story from far, far away. We're going to talk about a country called Iran. It's a place with its own special people and rules, just like our countries here in Africa. Sometimes, news can be a little bit sad, but it's important to learn about the world around us. Today, we have some news that makes us think about fairness and how different countries work. It's a story about two people in Iran who faced a very, very serious problem. Let's learn about it together, in a way that helps us understand.

What Happened Far Away in Iran?

In the country of Iran, there were two men named Abolhassan Montazer and Vahid Baniamerian. They were part of a group of people who had different ideas about how their country should be run. Imagine if you and your friends had different ideas about the best way to play a game – well, these people had different ideas about their country's rules. The government in Iran did not agree with this group. It told the group that they were not allowed. Because Abolhassan and Vahid were part of this group, they faced big trouble. They were caught and brought before important judges. These judges looked at what Abolhassan and Vahid had done. And then, a very sad thing happened. The judges made a final decision that led to Abolhassan and Vahid receiving the most serious punishment a country can give. This punishment is called an 'execution', and it means they lost their lives because of the country's laws. It's a very sad and serious thing to hear.

What Are Laws and Why Do We Have Them?

Every country, and even our schools and homes, has rules. We call these rules 'laws' when we talk about a whole country. Think about the rules in your classroom, like 'don't run inside' or 'always share your toys.' These rules help everyone stay safe and happy. Laws are like very important rules for everyone in a country. They tell people what they can and cannot do. Laws help to keep order and make sure people live together peacefully. For example, there are laws that say you should not take things that don't belong to you, or that you should not hurt other people. **Did you know?** The very first written laws were made thousands and thousands of years ago! People have always needed rules to live together. Sometimes, when people break the laws, there are punishments. These punishments are meant to teach people a lesson and to keep everyone else safe. In some countries, the punishments can be very strict, and in other countries, they might be different.

Different Countries, Different Rules

The world is a very big place, full of many different countries. Each country is like its own big family, with its own special ways of doing things. Just like our families have different rules, so do countries. What is allowed in one country might not be allowed in another. Iran is one of these countries. It is very far away from us, in a part of the world called Asia. The laws in Iran are sometimes very different from the laws in our countries here in Africa. Some countries have laws that are very, very strict, especially when people disagree with the government. This is why it's so important to learn about other places, even if the news is a little hard to understand. **Did you know?** There are about 195 countries in the whole world! Each one has its own flag, its own people, and its own rules! It's important to remember that not all countries have such strict punishments for people who disagree with the government. Many countries believe that everyone should be able to share their ideas, even if those ideas are different from the government's. This is called 'freedom of speech', and it's a very important idea in many places.

What Does 'Government' Mean?

When we talk about 'the government,' we mean the group of people who are in charge of running a country. They make the laws, decide how money is spent, and try to keep everyone safe. It's like the head teacher and teachers in your school who make sure everything runs well. Every country has a government. Sometimes, people in a country might think the government is doing a good job. Other times, people might think the government could do things better or differently. When people have different ideas, it's called 'disagreeing'. In many countries, people are allowed to share their disagreements peacefully, and they can try to change things in a fair way, like by voting for new leaders. But in some countries, like in this story about Iran, it can be very difficult and even dangerous to disagree with the government. This is what happened with Abolhassan and Vahid.

Why This News Makes Us Think

This news from Iran is sad because two people lost their lives. Many people around the world, including people in our own African countries, believe that everyone should be treated fairly and kindly. We believe that people should have a chance to say what they think, and that punishments should not be so severe. It's a reminder that not all places in the world are the same, and not all countries follow the same ideas about fairness and justice. In many African countries, we try to solve problems and disagreements in ways that do not involve such extreme punishments. We value talking things through and finding peaceful solutions.

Learning About the World Together

Even though this news is serious and a bit sad, it's important that we learn about it. Learning about different countries and their ways helps us understand the big, wide world we live in. It helps us think about what we believe is fair and kind. As your friendly reporter from Africa, I hope this story helps you think about rules, fairness, and how we can all work towards a more peaceful world where everyone is treated with kindness. Remember to be kind to each other, listen to different ideas, and always try to find peaceful ways to solve problems. Let's all hope for a future where such sad news becomes less and less common, and kindness rules the day. Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends!
summary: Two men in Iran, Abolhassan Montazer and Vahid Baniamerian, faced a very serious punishment called execution because they disagreed with their country's government.
whyMatters: This news is important because it shows us that different countries have very different laws and ways of punishing people, and it reminds us to think about fairness and kindness for everyone in the world.

originalTitle: Afghanistan earthquake kills eight members of same family
title: A Brave Little Boy and Helping Hands in Afghanistan
content:
Hello, Friends, from Your African News Reporter!

Today, we have a very special story from a country far, far away called Afghanistan. It's a story about strength, kindness, and how people help each other when times are tough. Even though Afghanistan is very far from Africa, we are all friends on Earth.

When the Earth Shakes

Sometimes, the ground under our feet can shake. It's like a giant rumble or a big wobble! When the ground shakes strongly, we call it an ‘earthquake’. Imagine you are playing with your toy cars, and suddenly the table starts to wiggle. That's a bit like what an earthquake feels like for the Earth. Did you know? Our Earth is like a big puzzle made of many pieces called 'plates'. Sometimes these pieces move a little bit, and that's when we feel a shake!

News from Afghanistan

A little while ago, a part of Afghanistan had a big earthquake. Many homes and buildings were shaken very, very hard. It was a scary time for the people living there. When homes shake so much, they can get broken. People can get hurt, and they might need a lot of help. This is a time when friends, neighbours, and people from all over the world come together to lend a hand.

Meet a Brave Little Boy

In one of the towns in Afghanistan, there lived a little boy. He was at home when the big earthquake happened. His home was shaken very badly. He got hurt a little bit during the big shake. It must have been very scary for him. Sadly, his family faced a very, very sad time. His mommy, daddy, brothers, and sisters are not with him anymore. He is the only one from his home who is still here. But this little boy is incredibly brave and strong. He is a true superhero for being so strong through such a difficult time.

Doctors and Kind Helpers

When the earthquake happened, many kind people rushed to help. Doctors and nurses came quickly to look after everyone who got hurt. They are like real-life superheroes with their stethoscopes and bandages! They gave the little boy medicine to help his boo-boos feel better. They made sure he was warm and safe in a hospital bed. Did you know? Doctors and nurses work very hard to make sure we all stay healthy and get better when we are sick or hurt. They have very kind hearts! Other helpers, like rescue workers, also came. They helped to find people and make sure everyone was safe. They are very brave too.

Getting Better Every Day

Our brave little boy is now getting better and better every single day. The doctors and nurses are taking very good care of him. He is surrounded by kind people who want to make him smile again. They give him warm food, comforting blankets, and lots of love. Even though he has been through a very sad and scary time, he is getting stronger. We hope he feels happy and safe very soon.

Kindness from Far Away

Even though we live in Africa and this little boy lives in Afghanistan, we can still think of him. We can send him our good wishes and hope he gets all the love and help he needs. It shows us that kindness has no borders. It means that no matter where you are from, you can be kind to others. When sad things happen, it's wonderful to see how people from all over the world come together to help. It reminds us that we are all part of one big global family.

What Can We Learn?

This story teaches us to be brave, just like the little boy. It also teaches us the importance of being kind and helping others, especially when they need it most. We can always try to be helpful to our friends, our family, and even people we don't know yet. A smile, a helping hand, or a kind word can make a big difference. Let's send our warmest thoughts to the little boy in Afghanistan and to all the kind people helping him. We hope for many happy and peaceful days ahead for him. Remember, even small acts of kindness can make the world a better place!
summary: A brave little boy in Afghanistan was hurt when the earth shook, but many kind people are now helping him to feel safe and get better every day.
whyMatters: It's important to know that even when sad things happen far away, people from all over the world come together to help each other and show great kindness.

originalTitle: NASA unveils first images of Earth from Artemis II
title: Look! Our Earth from Space!
content:
Hello, super kids of Africa! Get ready for some amazing news from way, way up high! Imagine looking at our beautiful Earth from a very, very far place. That's what some brave astronauts did, and they took pictures! They showed us these pictures for the very first time. It's like a special gift from space!

Our Brave Friends in Space

Some very brave people are flying in a big rocket ship. We call these people astronauts. They are like super explorers, but they explore space! Right now, they are flying far, far away from Earth. They are going on a super journey towards our friend in the sky, the Moon! These astronauts are on a mission called Artemis Two. It's a big, big adventure! They are flying in a special spaceship. This spaceship is like a tiny home that floats in space. They have cameras with them, ready to take amazing pictures.

A Journey to the Moon

The astronauts are not just going for a joyride. Oh no! They have an important job. Their job is to fly around the Moon. This trip helps them learn many new things. They are getting ready for even bigger journeys in the future. Maybe one day, they will even go to Mars! Mars is another planet, a bit red. Going to the Moon is a very long trip. It takes many days to get there. Imagine sitting in a car for days and days! But these astronauts are in a rocket, flying super fast. They are seeing things no one on Earth can see every day.

Our Blue Marble from Afar

So, what did these astronauts see? They saw our home, Earth! But not like we see it from our windows. They saw Earth from very, very far away. And guess what? Earth looks so beautiful from space! The pictures they sent show our Earth as a big, round, blue ball. It looks like a giant, shiny marble floating in the dark, dark space. We can see big white swirly clouds. These clouds are like fluffy blankets covering parts of our planet.

Did You Know?

* Did you know that Earth is mostly covered in water? That's why it looks so blue from space! The oceans are huge. * Did you know space is completely dark? There is no light from the sun bouncing off dust like here on Earth, so it looks black. * Did you know that the Moon is Earth's only natural friend in space? It goes around and around our Earth all the time.

Why Do They Take Pictures?

Taking pictures from space is very important. It helps scientists learn more about our planet. They can see how clouds move. They can see how our Earth changes. These pictures help us understand our home better. The astronauts also take pictures to show us, the people on Earth, what our planet looks like from far away. It reminds us that we all live on this one beautiful planet. It reminds us to take care of it.

Our Home, Planet Earth

When you look at the pictures, you might wonder where Africa is. Africa is a very big part of that blue and green ball. Our continent is one of the amazing places on Earth. We all share this one special home. These pictures make us feel connected. Even though the astronauts are far away, they are taking pictures of our shared home. It's like they are sending us postcards from space! How cool is that?

Who Sent Them? Meet NASA!

So, who are these amazing astronauts working for? They work for an organization called NASA. NASA is like a super big space school and workshop in a country far away called America. NASA helps build rockets and spaceships. They train astronauts to go to space. NASA wants to explore the universe. They want to learn all about the Moon, Mars, and beyond! They want to discover new things and share them with the whole world, including us in Africa.

Looking Up at the Moon from Africa

When you look up at the night sky from your home in Africa, you can see the Moon. It shines brightly for us. Now, imagine that our brave astronauts are flying around that very Moon! One day, maybe one of you super kids reading this will become an astronaut. Maybe you will fly to the Moon too! Or maybe you will be a scientist who helps build rockets. These pictures show us that dreams can fly very high, even to space!

More Fun Facts!

* Did you know astronauts wear special suits? These suits help them breathe and stay safe in space, where there is no air! * Did you know that when astronauts float in space, it's called 'zero gravity'? It means things don't fall down, they just float! * Did you know that the Moon does not make its own light? It gets its light from the Sun, just like Earth!

A Special Message from Space

These pictures are more than just photos. They are a message. A message that says, "Look how amazing our Earth is!" A message that says, "We are all together on this one special planet." It's a reminder to be kind to our Earth and to each other. Because from space, there are no borders, just one beautiful blue and white home. Let's keep looking up at the sky and dreaming big, just like these astronauts! This news makes us feel excited and proud. Proud of our beautiful Earth and proud of the amazing things humans can do.
summary: Brave astronauts on a journey to the Moon sent us amazing first pictures of our Earth, showing it as a beautiful blue ball from far away.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows us how beautiful our home planet Earth looks from space and inspires us to learn more about our universe.

originalTitle: Rescue team in Iran face 'harrowing and dangerous' search for US crew member
title: Brave Helpers Look for a Friend Far Away!
content:
A Big Search Begins!

Hello everyone! We have a special story today about some very brave people. These people are like real-life superheroes! They are on a big and important search right now. They want to find a special helper who is lost. It is a very serious job, but they are doing their best.

Where are the Helpers Searching?

This big search is happening in a country far, far away from us. This country is called Iran. It is a place with very big mountains and sometimes the weather can be very tricky. Imagine a giant playground, but with huge rocks, deep valleys, and lots of wind and clouds!

Who Needs Help?

Someone very kind is lost. This person is a helper from another country, a long way from Iran. We can think of them as our friend who needs help. They were doing important work to help others. But now, they are somewhere difficult to find.

Who are the Brave Rescuers?

Many kind and brave people are looking for our friend. They are called a rescue team. A rescue team is like a super team of helpers! They work together like a big family. They use special tools and their smart brains. They never, ever give up!

What is a Rescue Team?

Did you know? A rescue team is made up of many different people. Some are like doctors who know how to help someone who is hurt. Some are like police officers who know how to search big areas. Some know how to climb big mountains. They all want to find people who are in trouble and bring them to safety. They are super smart and super strong!

Why is it Hard?

Finding someone lost can be very, very hard. Imagine trying to find a tiny toy in a giant, giant field at night! It is even harder when the weather is not nice. Sometimes it is very cold, like when you see frost in the morning. Sometimes it is very dark, especially high up in the mountains. The mountains can be very big and rocky, making it hard to walk.

What Do Rescuers Look For?

A very smart person, who knows a lot about finding lost people, told us something important. He said the first thing helpers do is look for “signs of life.” This means they want to see if our friend is still breathing. They want to see if they can move or make a sound. They want to know if they are okay. They want to know if they can help them feel better right away. It’s like when you check if your little brother is still sleeping soundly.

Did You Know About Rescue Dogs?

Did you know? Some rescue teams have very special dogs! These dogs are super sniffers! Their noses are amazing. They can smell things that people cannot, even from far away. They can help find someone even if they are under snow or rocks, or hidden by trees. Woof woof! They are truly amazing helpers and very good friends!

How Do They Search?

The rescue team looks everywhere with great care. They walk over big rocks. They climb up hills that are very steep. They look under trees and behind big stones. They use their strong eyes and their good ears. They listen very carefully for any sounds, like a little cough or a whisper. They call out names loudly. They are very, very careful with every step.

Working Together is Key!

Did you know? When people work together as a team, they are much stronger and smarter! The rescue team is a big team. Everyone has an important job. Some people drive special cars that can go anywhere. Some fly high in helicopters. Some walk on their feet, searching every tiny bit of ground. They all help each other, like gears in a clock, to make the search work.

What is a Helicopter?

A helicopter is like a giant, noisy bird that flies high in the sky! It has big blades on top that spin around very fast. Helicopters can fly to places where cars cannot go, like the top of a big mountain or a very deep valley. They can look down from above. This helps them see a very wide area at once. They are very important tools for big searches!

The Weather Can Be Tricky

The weather in the mountains can change very, very fast. One minute it might be sunny and bright. The next minute, big, grey clouds can come quickly. It can get very windy, making it hard to walk and see. It can get very, very cold, like when you need to wear many warm clothes. This makes the brave rescuers' job even harder. They have to be strong and warm and ready for anything!

Sending Hope

Even though the search is hard, everyone has hope. Hope means we believe that something good will happen. We hope our friend will be found soon. We hope they will be safe and warm. Many people around the world, including us, are thinking about the rescuers and our lost friend. We are sending them happy and strong thoughts.

Why Do People Help?

Did you know? People help each other because they are kind. They care about others, even if they don't know them. When someone is in trouble or needs help, it makes us want to lend a hand. It's like when your friend falls down and you help them get back up. It's the same, but on a bigger scale, for everyone in the world!

Never Give Up!

The rescue team will not give up. They are very brave and very determined. They know that every minute counts when someone is lost. They will keep searching until they find our friend. They are trying their very, very best, showing us what real courage looks like.

What Happens When Someone is Found?

When someone is finally found, it is a very happy moment for everyone! Doctors and nurses are always ready to help. They can check if our friend is hurt or if they need anything. They can give them warm blankets and warm food. They will make sure they are okay and help them feel better.

A Message of Kindness

This story reminds us all to be kind to each other. It reminds us to help our friends and neighbours. It reminds us that even when things are tough, there are always brave people ready to help. Let's send our happy thoughts to the rescue team and our lost friend, hoping for a very happy ending!
summary: Brave rescue teams are working very hard in a faraway country to find a kind helper who is lost, hoping to bring them home safely.
whyMatters: It matters because it shows us how important it is for people to help each other, no matter where they are from, and to never give up hope when someone needs us.

originalTitle: Artemis II crew now halfway to Moon as they take 'spectacular' image of Earth
title: Our Friends Are Halfway to the Moon! And They Took a Super Picture of Earth!
content:
Hello, Space Explorers!

Hi everyone! Guess what? Something super exciting is happening far, far away, in outer space! Imagine brave people flying in a giant rocket, going on an amazing adventure. They are called astronauts. They are flying to our friend, the Moon! It's like going on the biggest trip ever! We get to hear all about it right here. Let's learn about their incredible journey. It's news for all of us, from the big cities to the smallest villages across Africa. When you look up at the night sky, you might see the Moon. And our space friends are going there! It makes us all wonder about the stars.

Our Brave Friends in Space

Meet our space friends! Their names are Reid Wiseman and his team. Reid is like the captain of their spaceship. He is very brave. He and his team are called astronauts. Astronauts are super special people. They train for many, many years to learn how to fly rockets and live in space. They wear cool helmets and special suits. It helps them stay safe in space, where it's very cold and empty. They are on a mission called Artemis II. It is a big mission! They are exploring space for all of us, for all humankind. * **Did you know?** Astronauts float in space because there is no gravity! It's like being a superhero, floating around your room!

What is Artemis II?

Artemis II is not just one rocket. It's a whole big plan! It's a plan to send people back to the Moon. Many years ago, people went to the Moon. Now, we want to go again! This time, we want to learn even more. Artemis II is taking astronauts on a trip around the Moon. They won't land this time. They will fly very close to it and then come back home to Earth. It's like a practice trip for future missions. They are learning how to travel safely in deep space. They are testing everything to make sure it's safe for future journeys. * **Did you know?** The first person to walk on the Moon was Neil Armstrong! That was a long, long time ago, in 1969.

Meet Orion, Their Space Home!

Our astronauts are flying in a special spaceship. It is called the Orion capsule. Think of Orion as their super-fast, super-safe car in space! It's not like a car you drive on the road. It's a very strong house that floats in space. The Orion capsule is where the astronauts live. They eat, sleep, and work inside it. It protects them from the cold and dangerous things in space. It has big windows so they can look out at the sparkling stars and distant planets. Imagine looking out of your window and seeing Earth as a small ball! It must be an amazing view. * **Did you know?** The Orion capsule is built to travel farther than any human spacecraft has gone before! It's designed for long trips.

A Long Trip to Our Moon Friend

Our space friends are on a very long journey. They are traveling very far from Earth. Right now, they are halfway to the Moon! Wow, halfway! That means they have flown a very, very long way already. The Moon looks small in the sky, but it's actually very far away. It takes many days to fly there. They are going super-duper fast! They fly past sparkling stars and big, empty space. It's a journey filled with wonder. They are exploring a path no one has taken in a very long time. This trip is an important step for future space travel. * **Did you know?** The Moon is about 384,400 kilometers away from Earth! That's like driving around the whole Earth 9 times, very, very fast!

Earth: A Beautiful Blue Marble!

Guess what amazing thing happened? Commander Reid Wiseman took a picture! He took a picture of our home, Earth, from his spaceship. He said it was "spectacular." That means it was incredibly beautiful! Imagine seeing our world from way, way up high. Earth looks like a big, beautiful blue marble floating in the dark space. You can see the big blue oceans and the fluffy white clouds. You can also see the green and brown land. All of Africa, all your homes, all your friends are on that tiny, beautiful blue marble. It must have felt amazing for them to see it. It reminds them of home and how special our planet is. * **Did you know?** Earth is called the "Blue Planet" because more than 70% of its surface is covered by water! We have so much ocean!

Why Are They Going to the Moon?

Why do these brave astronauts go on such a big trip? They are going to the Moon to learn new things! Scientists want to know more about the Moon. They want to know how it was made. They also want to learn if we can live on the Moon one day. This mission will help us prepare for even bigger trips. Maybe one day, people will go to Mars, the "Red Planet"! Going to the Moon helps us practice for those long journeys. They are making amazing discoveries for all of us. This journey helps us understand our solar system better and where we fit in it. * **Did you know?** The Moon has huge craters, which are like big holes, from when space rocks hit it a long, long time ago!

Dream Big, Future Explorers!

This exciting journey reminds us that anything is possible! Maybe one day, one of you, a kid from Africa, will become an astronaut. You could be flying to the Moon or even Mars! Keep looking up at the sky. Keep dreaming big dreams. Learn about science and space. Who knows what amazing things you will discover? The astronauts on Artemis II are showing us that if we work hard and dream big, we can reach the stars. They are inspiring children all over the world, including all of you in Africa, to think about the wonders of space and what you can achieve! * **Did you know?** There are many telescopes in Africa that scientists use to look at the stars and planets! Maybe you can visit one someday!
summary: Brave astronauts on the Artemis II mission are flying to the Moon in their special spaceship, Orion, and have taken a beautiful picture of our home, Earth, as they are halfway there.
whyMatters: This is important because it teaches us new things about space and the Moon, and it inspires all of us, especially kids in Africa, to dream big about exploring the stars and becoming future scientists or astronauts.

originalTitle: After 16 years in power, could Viktor Orban finally be unseated?
title: Big Election News from Far Away!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends all across Africa! Your kid-friendly news reporter is here with some exciting news from a country far, far away. Imagine a place with beautiful castles and yummy foods, a place called Hungary! It's in a part of the world called Europe.

Hungary is Getting Ready!

Guess what? In just a few days, something very important is going to happen in Hungary. It's like a really big game day, but instead of playing sports, the grown-ups are going to make a very important choice. They are getting ready for something called an election! Did you know? Hungary is a country in Europe, just like how Kenya or Nigeria are countries in Africa! Every country has people who help it run.

Who is Viktor Orban?

For a very, very long time, Hungary has had the same leader. His name is Viktor Orban. Can you imagine doing the same thing for a very, very long time? Mr. Orban has been the leader for 16 whole years! Think about how old you are. Maybe you are 7 or 8 years old. Mr. Orban has been the leader for longer than most of you have been alive! That's a super long time to be in charge of a country. He is like the captain of a big team, and his team is the whole country of Hungary. He makes big decisions about schools, hospitals, roads, and many other things that help people in Hungary.

What are Elections?

So, what happens at an election? It's a special day when all the grown-ups get to choose their leader. It's like when your class gets to choose a class monitor, or when a football team chooses its captain. Everyone gets to say who they think would be the best person to lead. People go to special places called 'polling stations.' It's a bit like a big voting booth. They look at the names of different people who want to be the leader. Then, they secretly choose the person they want. It's like a secret ballot, so nobody knows who you chose, which is fair. Did you know? The idea of voting is very old! Even in ancient times, people sometimes chose their leaders. It's a way for everyone to have a voice.

16 Years is a Long Time!

When a leader stays in power for 16 years, it means they have been making big decisions for a very, very long time. Some people in Hungary think this is good, because they like what Mr. Orban has done. They feel happy with his plans. But other people in Hungary might feel that it's time for a change. They might want someone new to try out different ideas. They might think a new leader could make things even better for Hungary. Imagine if you played the same game every single day for 16 years! You might want to try a new game, right? That's how some people feel about having a leader for such a long time.

Can Viktor Orban Be Unseated?

The big question now is: can Viktor Orban be 'unseated'? This means, can he be voted out of his job as leader? Or will the people of Hungary choose him again for another term? When we say 'unseated,' it means that the people might choose a different person to be their new leader. It's like when a new captain is chosen for a team. The old captain steps down, and a new one steps up. This is why the election is so exciting! Everyone is waiting to see what the people of Hungary will decide. Will they keep the same leader, or will they choose someone new?

Why Choices are Good

Having choices is very important in a country. It means that people can decide who they think will do the best job. When there are different people who want to be the leader, they can all share their ideas and plans. Then, the people get to pick the ideas they like the most. It's a way for everyone to feel like they are part of how their country is run. This is called 'democracy,' which means the people have the power to choose. Did you know? In many countries across Africa, grown-ups also go to the polls to choose their leaders. It's a special right that many people have.

What Happens Next?

In just nine days, the grown-ups in Hungary will go to those polling stations. They will cast their votes, which means they will secretly pick their choice. Then, all the votes will be counted very carefully. Soon after, we will know if Viktor Orban will stay as the leader of Hungary, or if a new person will step into that very important job. It's a big moment for Hungary and its people. It reminds us that everyone has a voice, and together, people can make big decisions for their countries. And that's your news update from far away Hungary! Keep learning, my friends! From your friend, the African news reporter for kids!
summary: In Hungary, a country far away in Europe, grown-ups are getting ready for a big election in just nine days to decide if Viktor Orban, who has been their leader for 16 years, will stay in power or if someone new will be chosen.
whyMatters: This is important because elections let people choose who they want to lead their country, and after a very long time with one leader, it's a big decision for everyone in Hungary whether to keep him or pick someone with new ideas.

title: Arteta defends Arsenal's international withdrawals
content: Mikel Arteta says Arsenal have '"a very good relationship" with most national teams, despite 11 of his players withdrawing from international duty.

originalTitle: Guardiola wants Rodri to stay but says unhappy stars can go
title: Guardiola wants Rodri to stay but says unhappy stars can go
content: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says unhappy players can leave the club, but he hopes midfielder Rodri will stay.

title: After more than 14 years at Atletico Madrid, what next for Simeone?
content: BBC Sport asks if Atletico Madrid could really part ways with manager Diego Simeone at the end of the season?
